reviews.review_text:
I haven’t played the first Ori so maybe my opinion doesn’t mean much, but I really loved this game. The visuals were beautiful, the music was amazing and moving around the map became so much fun once you unlocked more abilities. Some boss fights were annoying and took me a few tries but somehow I still had fun with it. The story was really nice and the ending actually got me emotional as well, wasn’t expecting that at all, honestly.
Highly recommended! 10/10

Ori and the Will of the Wisps is a successful sequel to The Blind Forest, although it plays things too safe for my tastes. The gameplay, exploration, and environments are all highly polished extensions of the original, but the plot and characters don't do anything new or interesting. It's a good game, but not particularly meaningful.

The best part of Ori is your character's movement. It really makes you feel like an evasive sprite: quick and cunning, but not powerful. As a sequel, WotW is also not afraid to let loose with the powerups. It gives you, like, a gajillion kinds of jumps and dashes. When you get the hang of these moves, you can cover some serious ground. It feels great. I especially liked the optional time trials that encourage you to practice the movement.

Combat feels like an extension of the movement in that it mostly involves you spamming a quick attack while darting in and out of range using your abilities. I liked that; it's yet another reward for mastering an excellent movement system. I did feel that it was a bit too easy to take cheap damage; probably because you are so fast, enemy attacks need to come out instantly to stand a chance of hitting you. Worse, it's sometimes just plain hard to see what's going on because of all the overlapping bright, flashing effects.

The environments are gorgeous, I just wish they stuck around for longer. The game felt a bit short, even to 100% complete. There are several in-game tools that help you find all the secrets, though, so maybe there's just less wasted time. The music in the stages was sadly not nearly as good as the original. The tracks all felt like they were merely trying to imitate what made The Blind Forest special, but they never got it right.

I couldn't care any less for the plot or characters this time around. The Blind Forest had a surprisingly emotional impact on me, but this time I didn't feel anything. I think it's because everything in WotW felt completely safe and predictable. Like, obviously the little owl character isn't going to die. Why try making me feel sad about it? It also really doesn't help that the narration all sounds like it's being read by Jabba the Hutt.

So, Will of the Wisps was a fun experience for me, but not a memorable one. I think it's better than the original in terms of raw gameplay, but when I think about Ori, I will always remember The Blind Forest instead. WotW isn't bad, it's just very, very safe. I'd still recommend it. If you liked The Blind Forest, then I think you will find something to appreciate here.

I’ve been playing games for over 10 years now, across all kinds of genres, but lately most games just felt boring after a few hours. Nothing really kept me hooked anymore, so I was looking for something smaller to casually play solo and hopefully just enjoy gaming again.

That’s when I found Ori and the Will of the Wisps on Steam.

Honestly, I expected it to be just a small little game I’d play for a few evenings — but this game completely blew me away.

Everything about it is amazing. The story, the music, the atmosphere, the level design… you can really tell how much love and effort went into this game. The difficulty is also perfect in my opinion. On normal mode it never felt unfair or frustrating, but the boss fights were still challenging and super fun.

What surprised me the most was how emotional this game got at times. I didn’t expect to get so attached to the world and the characters, but some moments genuinely hit hard.

And the craziest part is that what I thought would be a “small game” ended up giving me around 20–30 hours of pure fun. I took my time with it, explored a lot, played a few hours every day, and honestly enjoyed every second.

This is probably the first game in years that reminded me why I fell in love with gaming in the first place.

I can seriously recommend this to anyone who just wants a beautiful singleplayer experience with a great story and gameplay that actually keeps you invested.

A huge thank you to Moon Studios for creating this masterpiece.

Most sequels do not live up to their initial titles, but Ori and the Will of the Wisps manages to pull it off:
+ Visually and musically beautiful
+ Combat and platforming are a blast
+ Collectibles are satisfying to find
+ Different enough in the upgrades/equipment to keep things fresh

Приятная игрушка. Достойный прогресс после первой части. 
- Визуал на высоте 
- Уровень сложности головоломок и геймплейно (на среднем ур. сложности) мне подошёл - почти всё прошёл интуитивно без гугла 
- Звуковое сопровождение приятное 
- Спасибо за хаб и более живой окружающий мир)

Давно играл в первую, но не отпускает ощущение что сюжетно вторая часть для чуть более взрослой аудитории. Надеюсь проект был коммерчески успешен и студия нас обрадует следующим проектом

Personally, I'm not a fan of this sequel. I find myself backtracking at least 3/4 of the game time being played which is super annoying. The art is as good as the first, controls are fine and the boss battles are well done. It gets a positive overall but I can't see myself coming back for a sequel to this one. I think I'm retiring the series here.

Along with the first game, this is probably one of the most beautiful games ever made. The visuals, storytelling and soundtrack all come together in a perfect platformer that can, at least if playing on hardmode as i did, be a real challenge but also super fun as you dash around the open-world esque levels.

I'd strongly recommend playing this, but it should be played after the first game to get the full experience.
Even though this game took me many months to complete, even restarting midway through, the story is incredible, and the music and visuals are also amazing. 10/10 buy this game right now
its hard to say which ori is better because both are beautiful and enjoyable in all aspects. and cute. very cute.

Absolute masterpiece of a game, even better than the first in my opinion. The music, the visuals, the gameplay, everything is a 10/10.
Anyway, gem/10.

Best metroidvania sequel and it doesn't disappoints
- BGM and OST is amazing
- More platforming
- Combat skills and upgrades are okay-ish
- Some backtracking 
- Background and cutscene is beautiful
- A variety of upgrades to ensure originality

11/10 Tear jerking ending

The best Metroidvania game there is.

PRO
Challange: Perfect (solver problems at almost any time)
Design: Perfect (you could look at the light effects all day)
Battle: Perfect
Special habilities: almost Perfect (some you would want to use once only)
Music: Perfect
Characters: lovable
Ending: perfect
language: perfect


CONS
No counter. Thats the only thing I can think of... but you can get a reflect in the sword

There is so much I want to write. Maybe in a follow up. But this game tower above any other game, even shinobi or lost crown. I've seen people comparing it to hollow knight... Men. Hollow kingiht sucks in comparison. Ori and the will of the wisps lets you be creative and makes you feel like a god.

[i]Can a creature who has never known the light truly escape the darkness?[/i]

Enjoyed this one much more than the first game (probably because the Microsoft account didn't connect and I couldn't constantly check how many times I died while playing lol). Though simple, but still emotionally impactful story, some beautiful locations and fine quests, and, most importantly fun gameplay, which didn't seem as tedious as in the first part (perhaps, because there are more places to explore and upgrade yourself). Lovely time.

Ori and the Will of the Wisps is a sequel that feels quite different from Ori and the Blind Forest, yet it never loses the charm that made the original so special. In many ways, I would even say it surpasses its predecessor.

The story is once again beautiful, emotional, and memorable. While I personally prefer the tighter structure of the original, Will of the Wisps still delivers some of the most powerful moments in the series. If I had one complaint, it's that some story elements and characters felt a little underexplored, almost like there was cut content. Even so, the story remains one of the game's greatest strengths and stayed with me long after I finished it.

Just like the original, this game is a platforming heaven. Movement feels incredibly fluid, responsive, and rewarding, and I genuinely think the Ori series contains some of the best platforming in gaming. Traversing the world is often just as enjoyable as progressing through it.

The escape sequences also return and remain some of the most exciting and memorable moments in the game. They capture the same intensity that made them so special in Blind Forest and never fail to get your heart racing.

One of the biggest differences is its greater focus on combat. Unlike the first game, combat plays a much larger role here, and it is genuinely fun. The game offers a variety of weapons, abilities, and upgrades that allow you to approach encounters in different ways and develop your own playstyle. The shard system adds even more customization and makes experimentation rewarding throughout the adventure.

The world feels larger and more alive than before thanks to its side characters, side quests, and interconnected areas. The boss fights are another major highlight and a huge improvement over what the first game offered.

Combined with the breathtaking visuals, incredible animation, and phenomenal soundtrack, all of this comes together to create an unforgettable experience.

I absolutely love this game. It successfully evolves the formula of the original while preserving everything that made Ori special in the first place. In many ways, I think it surpasses Blind Forest, and it remains not only one of my favorite Metroidvanias, but one of my favorite games of all time.
It’s a great game! The second part upgraded everything from the original, including the gameplay, story, and look.

perhaps one of the best metroidvanias to be ever created, Ori and the will of the wisps takes everything good about the first game and improves on it in every capacity

the music is phenomenal, the levels are well designed and the world is absolutely stunning.

the bosses are extremely well designed with unique move sets and honestly a pretty difficult learning curve


ori is definitely not a game thats to be taken lightly 
it is by no means easy and thats what makes the game so special 

the story while simple is extremely heart touching and relatable with an extremely bittersweet ending

Ori is to this day one of those classics that deservs to be picked up at full price
Great game, just needs better controller support especially and specifically for like playstation controllers

Outside of the boss fights, I can agree this series remains beautiful. Very few issues were dropping the perfect 9 to an honest 8 and a half score. Mainly the beauty of the game and lore kept it within an 8 but the horrendous boss fights have to drop it a bit. I will admit this was better than Blind Forest and had slight improvements from it. 

Improvements could be saturation/brightness (it was hard to see Ori at times) and boss fights.

My biggest gripe is fighting bosses who hit boxes are hard to reach which should be an immediate thing games of this genre should avoid. It doesn't make the game hard/difficult, it makes it annoying and sad to even pursue completing the game.

I recommend because its still a great game and I enjoyed most of it.
Great game. You can tell just how much love the developers put into it. Highly recommend if you like cute stories and moderately challenging platformers!
Beautiful game, good vibe. The movement is fluid and satisfying

One of the best platformers I've ever played. I recommend this one and the first one as well.

My only complaint in this game is that sometimes they choose art direction over accessibility. You will spend a decent amount of time in boss battles wondering what hit you and how to avoid it.
It's alright. Hate the parkour but the movement in the game is pretty satisfying. Boss fights were lacking, and the escape sequences bordered annoying but I overall enjoyed the game and story.

This game was great! I liked Blind Forest and enjoyed WotW even more. I felt that they expanded and improved on the previous game and made it more balanced and fair. Blind Forest had some parts that I thought were overly difficult, but I didn't find that with WotW. I enjoyed the variety of abilities and environments. The story was a little predictable, but I don't think it was bad. I definitely recommend this game.

If you finished the first game and thought, "Man, I really wish the combat was meatier, the movement was faster, and I wanted to cry even more," then just buy this right now.

It is the textbook definition of a perfect sequel. It takes everything Blind Forest did right and somehow makes it vastly better. The addition of a proper melee combat system and actual boss fights is a massive upgrade, and chaining together dashes, grapples, and jumps makes you feel completely unstoppable. It is breathtakingly beautiful, the music is top-tier, and the ending will absolutely ruin you emotionally. 10/10, still crying.
Fantastic platformer, everything about it works.
GREAT movement and satisfying combat. Competing with hollowknight imo, even if it is more movement oriented

This game is a breath of fresh air....the story the mechanics the graphics, the colors the npcs and everything in between is so thoughtful that every second you play you feel like hug (despite challenging levels and complex acrobatics haha). Because the team at Moon have really thought through everything.

A beautiful game, and very fun too! I appreciate that this game features boss battles, unlike Ori and the Blind Forest, which only had chase scenes.
This is a worthy successor to the original game. Play it on the hardest difficulty by default :-)
Fun, beautiful and challenging game! Just like the Blind Forest.

If you are into fun metroidvania's, definetly pick this one up. The platforming and the abilities to do so are very good, the bash mechanic especially is very well integrated into the game. The game itself looks amazing, with good looking scenery. The game also gives you more of a enemy factor, than the first game, which is nice.

However, the music isnt the greatest, with only a couple of songs that I really think are memorable, but they do fit the biome you are exploring. The game's story feels also just like a revamp of the orginal game, which makes you not feel as immersed into the game. My main complaint is that the bossfights in this game are just bad, they feel like pushovers, and the ones you do struggle with are just bad gamedesign.

Still a fun title to pick up, if you love playing metroidvania's.

By far my favourite Metroidvania. Most other games of this genre are way too difficult for me to enjoy, but this game is just the right level of difficult, along with an amazing soundtrack, beautiful graphics, and incredibly satisfying movement, all helping bring the charming story to life.
Atmosphere, Levels, Story, Puzzles everthing is amazing. Music ❤️

The game brought back the child inside me. The story, the world, the view, the gameplay just took me back in time. All those feelings just rushed back and I enjoyed every moment of it when I was playing. I honestly can't remember the last time a game truely made me feel like this. It's been a while! :)
One of the best in the genre. Touching, magnificent art, lovely soundtrack. Improved a lot of things over the first game in the series. Very worth it.

I don't when the last time I played a "Metroidvania" type game. Probably when I was a little kid in the 90s. I'm 40 now with two kids of my own. I wasn't sure what I was getting into when I purchased this, but I was looking for something new and different. This game brough back feelings of a time when I played games as a child. It's one of those games you experience and know by the end that it's unique and there won't be anything else like it. I have to say I was very surprised. This was a lot of fun, challenging and bit frustrating for a person who doesn't play games like this often, but you get used to the mechanics. Finding and even upgrading abilities and powers was a lot of fun. The map is lush and fun to explore. The soundtrack is great, and goes with nearly every environment and story beat. At times, I felt like I was playing a modern day Mario Bros. I loved every minute and have to agree this is a masterpiece of a game.
Wish I could give this game more than just a positive review. S-tier platformer metroidvania and one of the finest examples of videogames as an art form
Great visuals, fun gameplay, and lovely story.

Great Sequel to an already amazing game, story, soundtrack and visuals are gorgeous.
Had an amazing time getting immersed into the world.

Elden Ring of Metroidvania, or maybe better, this game took my heart with it, and I loved it, I rate it 9.5/10 recommended for sure.

The 0.5 score because the annoying quest paths, it is not that helpful tbh, and there was some glitches and minor bugs, not game breaking and does not make you upset, but these preventing me from giving it perfect store.

Story top
Gameplay top
Sound top
Graphic top 

I liked that it has variety of play styles and more variants of enemies, I loved it.

Finally, for sure this is better than Ori and the Blind Forest (DE) but you have to play both, first Ori game is worth playing too 100%, and to understand the story here too, it is a kind of must play.
What else is there to say? Music? Fantastic. Art? Stunning. Story? Classical but very emotional. Combat? Miles better than blind forest. I'd recommend this to everyone.

Such a lovely game with great visuals, story and most of all music ! I usually find the sequal to a great game a bit sub-par but this one definitely overtook the first game in everything! I definitely enjoyed the side quests that got added as they were a fun little addition to the main story.

This game is so peak. Every single frame in this game is a painting. Combined with beautiful soundtracks, it creates an atmosphere that is both magical and heartbreaking. My favorite part is the boss chase sequences. Final score: 10/10 - An absolute masterpiece.

by far the most wonderful game i have ever laid my eyes upon. i thought gaming peaked at titanfall 2 but oh my god was i so utterly wrong. after playing blind forest, crying at the ending, and 100%ing it, i didnt think a sequel to a perfect game could exist. and after playing this game, not only was i blown away, i laid down and cried myself to sleep at the ending. not one moment of this game was spent with an ounce of anger or malice whatsoever, and all of the issues i had were resolved within minutes or even seconds of me speaking them out loud. i will never play a game as good as this ever again, and if i were to die after posting this review, my life will have been complete. 

and now i actually talk about the game: the graphics are so good, even in the first game, its hard to believe that these were made at the times they were. i didnt think this much effort could actually be put into JUST the art for the game, so thank you for blowing my mind in that regard!
the movement is exactly what i want out of a platformer game. after playing the first game, i felt naked walking around without the bash skill, and i was so excited when i started seeing those lanterns pop up near the tree. seeing the obstacles you need the skill to get past before getting the skill, then needing to backtrack to access those areas is just a chefs kiss.
the combat is WAY more immersive than the first game. oh my god. picking up the spirit blade felt so empowering by itself, to the point i was blown away yet again when i looked up a guide to the game and saw him swinging a hammer around. the multitude of options to choose from for magic abilities feels amazing, even if some of them may not be worth picking up.
the map upsize along with the change to magic and health felt AMAZING. it felt a little crowded at first, but getting deeper into the game, especially at the wellspring, seeing the huge open areas was amazing to traverse with the amount of movement you have. the health and magic change was VERY needed. fixing the damage and magic consumption felt way better, along with having fragments of cells that also fill your magic and health all the way up was awesome aswell.
the bosses and chases were much more dynamic, fit the theme of the game and area they were in, and felt way better to test out your skills and see how much better youve gotten. the addition to making ACTUAL bosses in this game opposed to just a few somewhat strong enemies in the first game was much needed. and having the fight go from either fighting to chase to fighting, or from a chase to fighting, no words. 
the addition of interactable characters and sidequests was VERY needed. so much so to the point i actually wanted more side quests by the end. 
the addition of more skills in the form of shards, and being able to fight in order to hold more shards, along with the ideas of the shards themselves and the implementation of them was a huuuge step up from the first game, well done.
the final fight felt SO amazing for such a packed game. it was perfectly as climactic as it needed to be, even through the times i died and i knew what was coming up. i loved being able to kill an irredeemable enemy like that, and the final blow felt so satisfying.
the ending was so on par with the story and the gameplay. i didnt want the ending to happen for 2 main reasons. first, i didnt want to stop being ori!! ori is such a fun character to play as and is also very sexy. the second reason is that i didnt want the game itself to be over. i know my skill cap, and i know i wont be able to get the achievements like beating the game in under 4 hours or beating it in one life, so this game has virtually no replay potential for me at least. BUT, in about 5 years when i forget about this game, i will come back and be amazed all over again. 

and now some personal notes about parts i liked: i dont know why im such a fan of this, but the environmental hazard of the spikes being so important in the game, that every area has a unique spike design is just SO amazing. that being said, I HATED MOULDWOOD. the flies buzzing around stuck to the walls almost made me throw up and i had to stop for a while to get past that. I personally think the first game had better spikes though.
the game being told from the perspective of ori after theyve transformed was such a huge punch in the gut. THAT is what made me cry as hard as i did. im not sure if the first game was also told like that, seeing as itd be confusing and continuity collapse. though i do wonder who was telling the first story...

in conclusion, i love this game. this is the best game ever made, and i refuse to change my opinion.
i know nobody will read this, but if you did, i love you too.

RIP KWOLOK <3
Great music and gameplay!

This game made me feel like I'm playing Hollow Knight again... I love it so much. The game is relatively easier than HK, which is still a good thing in a lot of ways. There's more focus on combat in Will of the Wisps compared to Blind Forest, but a lot of the focus is still in the unique platforming mechanics and fun level design. The world itself is visually stunning all while complemented by a beautiful sounding track, it's such a nice world to explore.
Great game, I felt it was a bit easier than the 1st game but overall a general step up in all aspects.
One of my favorite games ever. Everything about this game is beautiful. Can't recommend it enough
Its just as great as the first game. Love the platforming and different power ups.

The studio weren't afraid to re-invent themselves here - while it feels very similar, several old mechanics have been completely reworked. The movement feels smoother, the interesting but ultimately annoying self-saving mechanic is replaced with a classic checkpoint system, and you don't constantly lose progress if you forget to save or keep dying in the same place. One thing I don't love is the system where you can switch between different powerups all the time - this lets you customize the way you play but it's not my thing. I'm sure many will love it. The atmosphere in this game is even better than the first one, NPCs play a much bigger role, and the world feels as alive as ever. Runs smooth as butter on linux. One thing to note - easy mode in this game is significantly easier than in the first Ori. I regret starting on easy, as it doesn't feel quite challenging enough.

Ori and the Will of the Wisps is honestly one of the best games I have ever played. Everything about the game feels polished and made with so much care. The visuals are beautiful and every area looks like a painting.

The soundtrack is incredible and adds so much emotion to the story and world. Some moments genuinely hit hard because the music and atmosphere work so well together. The game feels emotional from beginning to end.

The movement is smooth and satisfying and the combat is a huge improvement over Blind Forest. Unlocking new abilities makes exploring even more fun and there is always something hidden to discover. The world feels alive and rewarding to explore.

What makes the game stand out for me is how memorable everything feels. The characters, environments, music, and gameplay all come together perfectly. It is one of those games that stays in your head long after finishing it.
one of the best game i ever played, everything is literally top tier
first game is good. second takes all the good from the first game and doubles down. however, very challenging to play.

It's a good game, (as I'm sure you know). There are many minor collision and UI oversights, as well as a few soft-locks if you don't do what the game expects you to do in certain sequences. Guides are kept to an intentional minimum, so you won't see any input prompts or arrows. Judging the depth of a scene is somewhat difficult, especially when they are on the same 3D plane so parallax doesn't play a card. This is especially apparent with hazards.

This game is such a piece of art, i literally cried when i finished it


(tho i had a lot of visual gliches throught the entie game, some gliching pixels in the background and my charakter somtimes shaking for no reason)

Not what I was expecting after playing the 1st Ori. Visuals look great but that is all I can say about this game. Everything is a chore. "Go here. Oh wait, you can't pass yet. Ok go here, oh did you speak to the hidden NPC behind a hidden wall? Ok cool. Now that you've done that, you'll need a special ability, go back to where you started." It feels as if the devs made this game with the idea of it being the most difficult/puzzle based game they could. The 1st Ori was a masterpiece but I cannot subscribe to this type of design. Combat feels weak and slow, most of the time you won't be able to see enemies because they blend with the environment. Stylistically that might be an upgrade from 1st Ori but personally, I don't like it. I can't with good faith recommend Ori 2

Like Blind Forest, WIll of the Wisps is visually brilliant. But unlike its predecessor, this game has a far more developed and engaging combat system, although even on hard you are never really challenged combat wise. Funnily enough though this combat system, while clearly necessary to keep the game competitive in the more saturated metroidvania market, makes the game more soulless than its predecessor? While Blind Forest was its unique statement, this game at times feels like it was developed to conform to what audiences deem 'necessary' in a 'great' metroidvania (a.k.a whatever Hollow Knight does). Still an awesome game though.

best graphics for 2d game with beautiful environment and level design 
 
story 8/10
graphics 10/10
level 9/10
parkour 9/10
characters 8/10

Sorry, best metroidvania graphics ever, best movement, one of my favorite combat system ever.
I won't go into more details, there are way better reviews than mine, I just realized I never left a thumbs up on this one.
Great sequel! Perhaps would be even better, had i not played the original :) 20 hours for 100% is perfect.
Wonderful Music and picture! clear instruction with puzzles
An amazing Metroidvania! It features a great variety of skills and areas that only become accessible as you unlock new abilities.

Well, I cant' actually recommend something I do not have all of, yet. When (if) I pay the extra $18 to get the rest of the game, I can then say whether or not I really recommend it but until then, how can I?

[h1]The game is beautiful but difficult to play.[/h1]
Let's start with the positives.  The background music is superb.  The graphics are very pretty to look at.  Some of the NPCs (the Moki, mostly) are cute & funny.
But I found it difficult to play this game, which made it hard to enjoy the experience.  I'm not talking about the challenges the game designers intentionally crafted, I mean that it is difficult to get the game working on my computer.
- The game is laggy on my potato computer even with the lowest graphics settings.
- The lag can be extremely frustrating in boss fights or timed sequences.
- The input system doesn't work by default with my non-Microsoft controller, so I had to fiddle around with third-party software to get it to work.  On a related note, there is no option in the options menu to swap between major brands of controllers, such as Xbox or PS, with different button configurations.  I don't care if Microsoft owns the game studio, their games should at least be compatible with popular/widespread controllers, even non-Microsoft-branded ones.
- The game takes forever to load--I must wait for a long time before the main menu will appear.
- The game takes forever to unload.  When I wish to stop playing, it takes a small eternity to reach the main menu again.  Then, when I select "exit game," it takes an eternity for the process to finally terminate.  I'm no expert, but it should not take this long.
- There are some frustrating physics quirks, such as enemies clipping through terrain (which sometimes renders combat challenges impossible to complete), or the character getting squished to death in situations that don't make sense to me, or the character being able to use their abilities on objects on the other side of locked doors.
- It can be difficult, at times, to distinguish interactable and non-interactable objects.  For instance, there is an ability which can melt ice, but it's not so easy to distinguish which pieces of ice can & cannot be melted in this way.
[hr][/hr]
Overall, the game would probably be a lot more enjoyable if my computer were beefier & if I had an Xbox-brand controller.  But I don't.

- Good story
- Good gameplay
- Good BGM
- Good color
  
   10/10
  Really good game !!
Art style too peak, saw the ultra detailed bug nest and had to turn down the graphics
story wise its the same game, but more combat focused
Ori series will always have special place in my heart. Absolute Masterpiece.

4/5 stars
Not as fun but worth a playthrough if you like the first one
